description: Introduce a Collections grouping layer above recipes with collaborative sharing, replacing per-recipe sharing with collection-level sharing

userStories.title: Create Collections Schema Migration
userStories.description: Create the collections table, collection_shares table, add collection_id to recipes, set up RLS policies, helper functions, indexes, and Realtime publication
acceptanceCriteria: collections table exists with columns: id (uuid PK), owner_id (uuid FK profiles ON DELETE CASCADE), name (text NOT NULL), emoji (text nullable), description (text nullable), is_default (boolean NOT NULL default false), sort_order (int NOT NULL default 0), created_at (timestamptz), updated_at (timestamptz)

collection_shares table exists with columns: id (uuid PK), collection_id (uuid FK collections ON DELETE CASCADE), shared_with_email (text NOT NULL), shared_by (uuid FK profiles), permission (text NOT NULL CHECK IN read/write default write), added_at (timestamptz)
Unique constraint on collection_shares(collection_id, shared_with_email)
recipes table has new collection_id column (uuid, nullable at this stage) with FK to collections(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
Index on collections(owner_id), collection_shares(collection_id), collection_shares(shared_with_email), recipes(collection_id)
RLS on collections: owner can CRUD; shared users (via collection_shares email match) can SELECT
RLS on collection_shares: collection owner can INSERT/DELETE; shared users can SELECT their own rows
RLS on recipes updated: shared users with write permission on the recipe's collection can INSERT/DELETE recipes in that collection
is_collection_owner() SECURITY DEFINER helper function works correctly — no RLS recursion
Both collections and collection_shares added to supabase_realtime publication
Migration applies cleanly on fresh database and existing production database
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Migration created at supabase/migrations/20260304210000_create_collections.sql. Creates collections + collection_shares tables, adds collection_id to recipes, 3 SECURITY DEFINER helpers, full RLS policies with transition support for recipe_shares, indexes, Realtime publication.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Schema + RLS policies are foundational — errors here cascade to every subsequent story

userStories.title: Data Migration — Existing Recipes to Default Collections
userStories.description: Migrate all existing recipes into owner-specific default collections, migrate recipe_shares to collection_shares, make collection_id NOT NULL, and drop/archive recipe_shares
acceptanceCriteria: For each distinct owner_id in recipes, a default collection is created (name='My Recipes', emoji='📖', is_default=true)
All existing recipes have collection_id set to their owner's default collection
For each distinct (shared_by, shared_with_email) pair in recipe_shares, a 'Shared Recipes' collection is created and shared recipes are moved into it with a collection_shares row
collection_id column on recipes is altered to NOT NULL after all recipes are assigned
recipe_shares table is dropped or renamed to recipe_shares_archive
Recipe count before and after migration matches — no data loss
Migration is idempotent (safe to re-run if collections already exist)
All operations run in a single transaction
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Migration at supabase/migrations/20260304220000_migrate_recipes_to_collections.sql. Creates default collections, assigns recipes, migrates recipe_shares to collection_shares, makes collection_id NOT NULL, changes FK to ON DELETE RESTRICT, archives recipe_shares table, updates all SELECT policies to collection-only sharing.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Irreversible production data transformation — must preserve all existing recipes and share relationships

userStories.title: Service Layer — Collection CRUD
userStories.description: Add collection CRUD functions to recipeDatabase.js: createCollection, updateCollection, deleteCollection, getCollections, subscribeCollections, ensureDefaultCollection, moveRecipeToCollection. Modify createRecipe to require collectionId.
acceptanceCriteria: createCollection(userId, { name, emoji, description }) returns new UUID
updateCollection(collectionId, { name?, emoji?, description? }) updates the row and sets updated_at
deleteCollection(userId, collectionId) deletes the collection — throws if it is the user's last collection
getCollections(userId) returns array of owned collections ordered by sort_order
subscribeCollections(userId, callback) delivers initial data and reacts to Realtime changes, returns unsubscribe function
ensureDefaultCollection(userId) returns existing default collection UUID or creates one if missing — idempotent
moveRecipeToCollection(recipeId, collectionId) updates recipes.collection_id and updated_at
createRecipe(userId, recipe) now requires collectionId parameter and fails without it
All functions use try/catch with contextual error messages and { cause } wrapping (match existing pattern)
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Added 7 new collection CRUD functions (createCollection, updateCollection, deleteCollection, getCollections, subscribeCollections, ensureDefaultCollection, moveRecipeToCollection) and modified createRecipe to require collectionId. All follow existing patterns.

userStories.title: Service Layer — Collection Sharing
userStories.description: Add collection sharing functions to recipeDatabase.js: shareCollection, unshareCollection, getCollectionShares, subscribeSharedCollections, addRecipeToSharedCollection, removeRecipeFromSharedCollection. Remove old per-recipe sharing exports.
acceptanceCriteria: shareCollection(collectionId, email) inserts a collection_shares row with permission='write', normalizes email to lowercase/trimmed
shareCollection rejects sharing with the collection owner's own email
unshareCollection(collectionId, email) deletes the collection_shares row
getCollectionShares(collectionId) returns array of { id, email, addedAt }
subscribeSharedCollections(email, callback) delivers collections (with recipe counts) shared with the given email via Realtime, returns unsubscribe function
addRecipeToSharedCollection(userId, collectionId, recipe) creates a recipe in the shared collection and returns UUID
removeRecipeFromSharedCollection(userId, recipeId, collectionId) deletes a recipe only if the current user added it (not the owner's recipes)
Old exports removed: shareRecipe, unshareRecipe, getRecipeShares, subscribeSharedRecipeRefs
All functions follow existing error handling patterns
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Added 6 collection sharing functions (shareCollection, unshareCollection, getCollectionShares, subscribeSharedCollections, addRecipeToSharedCollection, removeRecipeFromSharedCollection). Old per-recipe sharing functions kept with @deprecated tags — will be removed in US-012.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Collaborative write access via RLS requires careful permission boundary testing

userStories.title: RecipeContext — Collection State
userStories.description: Refactor RecipeContext to add collection state (collections, sharedCollections, activeCollectionId), collection actions (CRUD, sharing, selectCollection, moveRecipe), filter recipes by active collection, and replace per-recipe sharing with collection sharing
acceptanceCriteria: New state: collections (array), sharedCollections (array), activeCollectionId (string|null)
collections state populated via subscribeCollections on mount and stays in sync via Realtime
sharedCollections state populated via subscribeSharedCollections for the user's email
activeCollectionId defaults to the user's default collection (is_default=true) on first load
Selecting a collection updates activeCollectionId and clears activeRecipeId
recipes array only contains recipes for the active collection
New actions exposed: createCollection, updateCollection, deleteCollection, selectCollection, moveRecipe, shareCollection, unshareCollection, getCollectionShares
deleteCollection({ deleteRecipes: false }) moves recipes to default collection before deleting
deleteCollection({ deleteRecipes: true }) deletes all recipes then the collection
If the active collection is deleted, activeCollectionId falls back to the default collection
createRecipe passes activeCollectionId as the collectionId
Old actions removed: shareRecipe, unshareRecipe, getShares. Old state removed: sharedRecipes
All existing recipe actions (update, delete, upload image, update ingredients/steps) continue to work
Lint passes
userStories.testIntensityReason: Major state management refactor — must preserve all existing recipe functionality while adding collection layer

userStories.title: RecipeSelector — Collection List View
userStories.description: Replace the flat recipe list in RecipeSelector with a two-state sidebar. Default state shows collection list with owned and shared sections, emoji, name, recipe count, search, 3-dot menus, and + New button
acceptanceCriteria: Collection list is the default view when Recipes tab is selected
My Collections section shows owned collections with emoji + name + recipe count + chevron
Shared with me section shows shared collections with emoji + name + owner name + recipe count + Shared badge + chevron
Search bar filters collections by name
+ New button opens inline form or modal to create a collection (name + emoji picker)
3-dot menu on owned collections: Rename, Share, Delete
3-dot menu on shared collections: Leave (removes user's own access after confirmation)
Rename opens inline edit or modal
Delete shows confirmation dialog (US-015 handles full implementation, basic confirm here is fine)
Empty state: 'No collections yet. Create one to organize your recipes.'
Mobile: bottom sheet menus and full-screen list with sticky header
Desktop: dropdown menus and sidebar column
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes

userStories.title: RecipeSelector — Recipe List Within Collection
userStories.description: When a collection is selected, show recipe list within it with back navigation, search, recipe CRUD, Move to Collection action, and templates section
acceptanceCriteria: Back button returns to collection list view
Header shows collection name and emoji
Recipe list shows only recipes in the selected collection with thumbnail, name, ingredient/step counts, chevron, 3-dot menu
Search filters recipes by name within the collection
+ New Recipe button creates recipe in the currently selected collection
3-dot menu on owned recipes: Edit, Move to Collection, Delete
3-dot menu on recipes in shared collections added by current user: Edit, Remove from Collection
3-dot menu on recipes in shared collections added by others: View only
Move to Collection opens a collection picker showing all owned collections except the current one
Selecting a target collection moves the recipe and updates the list
Templates section (Browse Templates) remains functional at the bottom
Shared collection members can add new recipes (+ New Recipe visible)
Shared collection members can remove recipes they added
Mobile: iOS push/pop transition from collection list to recipe list
Desktop: sidebar smoothly transitions between states
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes

userStories.title: ShareCollectionModal — Collection-Level Sharing
userStories.description: Create ShareCollectionModal to replace ShareRecipeModal. Email-based sharing with add/remove collaborators, validation, portal rendering, and loading/error states
acceptanceCriteria: Modal opens when Share is selected from collection 3-dot menu
Header shows 'Share [Collection Name]'
Subtitle: 'People you share with can add and view recipes in this collection.'
On mount fetches existing shares via getCollectionShares(collectionId)
Email input + Share button adds collaborator — inserts collection_shares row and refreshes list
Validation: non-empty, valid email format, not the owner's email, not already shared
People with access list: owner with Owner badge, then each share with email + Remove button
Remove button deletes the collection_shares row and refreshes
Error states shown inline
Loading states during share/unshare operations
Rendered as portal to document.body
Close triggers: backdrop click, Escape key, X button
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes

userStories.title: App.jsx — Desktop Layout Wiring
userStories.description: Update renderRecipesView() and modal wiring in App.jsx: replace sharingRecipeId with sharingCollectionId, wire ShareCollectionModal, ensure all recipe flows work through collection context
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop recipes view renders the two-state collection sidebar (RecipeSelector)
Selecting a collection shows its recipes in the sidebar
Selecting a recipe from the sidebar shows its detail in the content area
ShareCollectionModal opens for collections (not individual recipes)
sharingRecipeId state replaced with sharingCollectionId
Creating a recipe adds it to the currently active collection
All existing recipe flows (edit, delete, image upload, add to list) continue to work
Template save creates recipe in the active collection
Desktop tab switcher (Lists/Recipes) remains unchanged
No regressions in the Lists view
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes

userStories.title: Mobile Navigation — Collection Drill-Down
userStories.description: Update mobile navigation to support 3-level drill-down: collection list → recipe list within collection → recipe detail. Update useMobileNav with openCollectionId state and browser history integration
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a collection pushes to the recipe list view with iOS slide transition (300ms)
Tapping a recipe pushes to detail view (existing behavior preserved)
Back button at each level works correctly: detail → recipe list → collection list
Browser back button navigates through the full 3-level stack
Tab switching returns to collection list (root of Recipes tab)
Transitions are smooth 300ms slides matching existing pattern
No flicker or layout shift during transitions
useMobileNav has new openCollectionId state for tracking active collection
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
userStories.testIntensityReason: 3-level nav stack with browser history integration and transition animations is complex state management

userStories.title: MobileRecipeDetail — Collection Context
userStories.description: Update recipe detail view to show collection name breadcrumb, add Move to Collection action in 3-dot menu, and remove per-recipe Share button
acceptanceCriteria: Collection name visible on detail view as breadcrumb or subtitle (e.g., 'in Weeknight Dinners')
Move to Collection action in 3-dot menu for owned recipes opens a collection picker
Per-recipe Share button removed from the recipe detail nav bar
Add to List functionality continues to work
Works on both mobile and desktop (MobileRecipeDetail is shared)
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes

userStories.title: Remove Per-Recipe Sharing Code
userStories.description: Delete ShareRecipeModal.jsx and .module.css, remove shareRecipe/unshareRecipe/getRecipeShares/subscribeSharedRecipeRefs from recipeDatabase.js, remove shareRecipe/unshareRecipe/getShares/sharedRecipes from RecipeContext, remove sharingRecipeId from App.jsx
acceptanceCriteria: ShareRecipeModal.jsx and ShareRecipeModal.module.css are deleted
recipeDatabase.js no longer exports: shareRecipe, unshareRecipe, getRecipeShares, subscribeSharedRecipeRefs
RecipeContext no longer exposes: shareRecipe, unshareRecipe, getShares actions or sharedRecipes state
No references to recipe_shares, shareRecipe, unshareRecipe, or ShareRecipeModal remain in application code
Build succeeds with no unused import warnings
Lint passes
userStories.testIntensityReason: Removing code across multiple files — must ensure no dead references remain

userStories.title: Default Collection Auto-Creation on Sign-Up
userStories.description: Ensure new users automatically get a default My Recipes collection on first sign-in via ensureDefaultCollection in RecipeContext
acceptanceCriteria: A brand-new user who has never created a recipe sees a 'My Recipes' default collection
Default collection created automatically on first load via ensureDefaultCollection(userId)
Existing users who went through data migration already have a default — no duplicate created
ensureDefaultCollection is idempotent
activeCollectionId is set to the default collection
Lint passes

userStories.title: Collection Emoji Picker
userStories.description: When creating or editing a collection, allow the user to pick an emoji using the same native emoji picker pattern from the lists feature (prd-native-emoji-picker)
acceptanceCriteria: Emoji picker appears when creating a new collection
Emoji picker appears when renaming/editing a collection
Selected emoji saved to collections.emoji column
Emoji displays next to collection name in the collection list
Default emoji for new collections: 📖 (user can clear it)
Reuses same emoji picker component/pattern as lists
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes

userStories.title: Delete Collection Confirmation Dialog
userStories.description: When deleting a collection with recipes, show confirmation dialog with two options: delete all recipes or move recipes to default collection. Empty collections get simple confirmation.
acceptanceCriteria: Dialog title: 'Delete [Collection Name]?'
If collection has recipes: two options — 'Delete collection and all [N] recipes' (destructive/red) and 'Move [N] recipes to [Default Collection Name] and delete collection' (safe/primary)
If collection is empty: simple confirmation with Delete and Cancel buttons
'Move recipes' option moves all recipes to default collection then deletes the collection
'Delete all' option deletes all recipes and the collection
If deleting the default collection: auto-create a new default collection first, move recipes there, then delete the old one
Cancel dismisses without action
Verify in browser
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
userStories.testIntensityReason: Multiple deletion paths including default collection edge case
